content: Combining the task-agnostic, no-training Self-Refine iterative improvement framework with multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms produces a new hybrid framework: Self-Refine that leverages perspective-aware self-contrast at each iteration to catch blind spots a single-perspective self-feedback would miss, while still retaining Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra supervised training or fine-tuning. This hybrid approach would address the key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine, which often gets stuck in local feedback loops where the model cannot recognize its own errors because it is evaluating output from the same single perspective that generated it.
